name: Stéphane Grappelli
abstract: Stéphane Grappelli (French pronunciation: [stefan ɡʁapɛli]; 26 January 1908 – 1 December 1997) was a French jazz violinist. He is best known as a founder of the Quintette du Hot Club de France with guitarist Django Reinhardt in 1934.

concerts.label: Stéphane Grappelli and the Diz Disley Trio / Ronnie Scott and His Trio

works.t: No No Nanette: Tea for Two
I Can't Give You Anything But Love

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: A Salute to Django Reinhardt
Absolut Grapelli: Back at Carnegie Hall
Absolut Grappelli: Fit as a Fiddle at 83

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: He was a classical musician that could play jazz also and had recorded some duets with the famous Stephane Grappelly in France.

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: His duo recordings with Stephane Grappelli... are among his favorites
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 23, 2003 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Arthur Briggs
about_quotes.evidence: I engaged Django Reinhardt... and Stephane Grapelly on piano-violin. Well, it so happened that in order for us to get a rest in the evening... Grapelly would play his violin and Django would play his guitar.

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: When I was sharing a dressing room with Stéphane Grappelli... I never had met him before... Stéphane said, 'Oh, c’est jolie, mon petit Toots.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: The 12th of November I recorded under my own name with Stephane Grappelli... Grappelli surprised me by playing piano on some of the numbers.

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: George has performed and recorded with a long list of jazz luminaries, including Ruby Braff, Stephane Grappelli and Buck Clayton.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001
